name: Skip The Final No-Refill CTA Sync On The Compact PTX Anchor

summary.name: Skip The Final No-Refill CTA Sync On The Compact PTX Anchor
summary.hypothesis: On the penultimate tile, the current compact PTX loop still pays cp_async_wait_group_0 plus __syncthreads even though future_tile_idx is already out of range and no shared stage will be overwritten. Skipping that final no-refill sync should remove one CTA barrier from the steady-state path without changing registers, stage depth, or the shared-memory budget.
summary.expected_bottleneck: The remaining local tax is unnecessary final-drain barrier overhead on the accepted compact PTX surface.
summary.risk: Low-to-moderate. This is a tiny control-path trim, but it still changes CTA synchronization semantics on the last steady-state handoff.
ranking_notes: This is the highest-signal next move because it isolates one measurable barrier seam on the accepted anchor without reopening any broader family.
The edit is small enough to keep the checkpoint round interpretable even if the gain is modest.
If this does not move the 24.68 ms base, the compact PTX barrier family is likely close to exhausted.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if removing the final sync causes any correctness mismatch or if the change grows into a broader steady-state refactor.
expected_local_changes: Special-case the penultimate-tile handoff so the loop skips the final no-refill __syncthreads.
Keep the compact 128x128 PTX dispatch, grouped_rows=4, and 22,016 B shared-memory footprint unchanged.
Do not combine this trim with any wait_group_1 or refill-before-wait rewrite.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
